title: C4ADS Exposes North Korea Sanctions Evasion via Shell Companies and Illicit Shipping Networks

contents:
## C4ADS Exposes North Korea Sanctions Evasion via Shell Companies and Illicit Shipping Networks

### Overview
C4ADS investigation uncovers North Korean shell companies facilitating sanctions evasion through illicit shipping networks. Report details ghost tankers and shadow fleet operations. Findings highlight persistent evasion tactics.

### Event Details
C4ADS launches comprehensive investigation into North Korea's sanctions evasion strategies, focusing on shell companies and illicit shipping networks. Researchers identify extensive use of ghost tankers—over 1900 vessels—that circumvent international restrictions. These ships transfer cargoes at sea, disguise origins, and employ deceptive practices to deliver sanctioned goods. Shell companies register under opaque jurisdictions, masking true ownership linked to Pyongyang entities. Illicit networks span multiple countries, coordinating vessel movements and financial flows to sustain regime revenue. C4ADS reports demonstrate how North Korea exploits lax port controls and complicit flag states. Investigators track specific vessels engaging in ship-to-ship transfers near high-risk areas. Analysis reveals patterns of insurance fraud and falsified documents. Organization urges governments to enhance monitoring and designate additional entities. Findings build on prior exposures, showing networks evolve rapidly to counter enforcement actions. C4ADS shares data with partners to disrupt operations. Report emphasizes urgency in targeting financial enablers and shipping facilitators. Investigators continue monitoring developments and updating intelligence.

### Source Context
@C4ADS (C4ADS) covers this category of developments. The account focuses on c4ads investigation north korea sanctions evasion shell company illicit network shipping.
